task_completion.description: Check if the agent opens and reads the specification document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Desktop/canard_pusher_spec.txt'.

task_completion.description: Check if the agent opens the OpenVSP application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenVSP main window and Geometry Browser are visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Check if a fuselage component is added to the model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A fuselage shape is visible in the OpenVSP 3D view, or a 'Fuselage' component is listed in the Geometry Browser.

task_completion.description: Check if both a main wing and a canard are added, with the canard positioned forward.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two wing components are visible in the 3D view. One wing (the canard) is positioned visibly ahead of the other (the main wing) along the longitudinal (X) axis.

task_completion.description: Check if at least one vertical surface (winglet or fin) is added.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A vertical aerodynamic surface, such as winglets on the main wing tips or a vertical fin on the aft fuselage, is visible in the 3D view.

task_completion.description: Check if the agent configures the wing parameters according to the specifications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenVSP geometry editing window is open for a wing component, showing parameters like Span or Sweep being adjusted (e.g., Main span 6.75, sweep 26.0; Canard span 3.05, sweep 14.0).

task_completion.description: Check if the agent saves the completed model to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog box in OpenVSP showing the file name 'canard_pusher.vsp3' and the directory '/home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent uses the OpenVSP GUI to construct the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenVSP interface being interacted with (adding components, changing parameters), rather than a text editor writing XML code for a .vsp3 file.

integrity.description: Verify the model is built progressively.
integrity.visual_evidence: The model starts empty or with a single component and progressively gains parts (fuselage, wings, canard) across multiple screenshots, rather than appearing fully formed instantly.

integrity.description: Verify the agent saves the file from within OpenVSP.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenVSP 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog is used to write the file, rather than terminal commands moving or renaming an existing file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The specification requires the main wing to have a span of 6.75m and a sweep of 26.0°, and the canard to have a span of 3.05m and a sweep of 14.0°. The final model must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/canard_pusher.vsp3.
